free_text: Energy dependence of elastic and inelastic
scattering from C12 for protons between 14 and 19 MeV
Palmer Physical Laboratory
Princeton FM cyclotron
Polystyrene targets of deltaE=l.6 MeV were used.
Various thin polystyrene and mylar foils were used
as targets.Commercial chemical analysis of these
foils showed only minor deviations, in the hydrogen
content, from their formulas Cn-Hn and C10-O4-H8.
The target thickness was always chosen to be smaller
than l00 keV.5 different thin targets were used
(commerical polystyrenr and mylar foils).
Conventional scintillation-counter
spectroscopy.Six NaI counters were mounted inside a
60-in, scattering chamber described previously and
used simultaneously whenever three multichannel
analyzers were available. Protons were detected with
a small(1-in.-diam, 0.1-in.-thick) NaI(Tl)crystal
(Harshaw Chemical Company), and pulses from the
elastic peak were spread over about 50 channels in a
multichannel pulse-height analyzer.
Normalizations and cross checks for the thick-target
excitation functions were obtained by conventional
thin-target cross section measurements.
Faraday cup, deltaE
All yielding absolute C12 cross section
values that agreed within 2%. Other effects like
counter geometry, multiple scattering in the thin
targets,the cut-off of the low-energy tails of peaks
for integration,slit scattering, and the 1%
abundance  of C13 in the targets lead to errors
smaller than the typical relative errors and were
neglected. The 7.66 MeV cross sections given,
therefore, contain unknown and possibly large
systematic errors and should be considered as
qualitative information only.
The angular calibration error is +-0.2deg.
